item.title: My worst nightmare
item.content:encoded: As a real-life hug lover and teddy bear collector, I am very glad that this thing exists only in fiction. A symbol of comfort and innocence creating evil, destructive, uncuddly dupicates of itself? It's enough to make my skin crawl; if I lived in the SCP universe and learned that #1048 existed, I'd want to neutralize that thing and its creations on sight. (Imagine a minor GoI dedicated to preserving harmless, innocent. and cute anomalies — and trained to recognize/eliminate evil impostors. "Snuggles Comfort People", their calling cards might read.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subject resembles a teddy bear similar in size and shape to SCP-1048, but is made entirely out of human ears.

Easily one of the most alarming lines on the entire wiki. Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: _cc tag removed per above.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apologies to the creator, but I do not care for the new image at all and was not consulted about it in any way. I am removing it and will see about finding a replacement I can live with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New image is creepy… and i like it

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: New image is by thepeachpeddler, under CC-BY-2.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really good post! The part with the baby was really nice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this one was written just to spite of all the SCPs that were allowed to roam freely around their facilities.

No, that was just happy coincidence. I was still pretty new at the time, so hadn't seen many "free-to-roam" SCPs yet, and wasn't aware it was becoming a tired trope at that point. I also wasn't familiar with much creepy pasta other than maybe Candle Cove at the time. Is it too edgy? Probably. But it apparently filled a niche that hadn't yet been filled at the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am reminded how much I dislike this SCP and how sloppily it is written. It very much has roots in old creepypasta tales on the internet and is trying too hard for those scares. REDACTEDs thrown in there without much sense and the site doesn't seem to be concerned that this killer bear is running around in the vents still? I know this is a classic and the standards were different then, so I'm being extra harsh on this because at 1114+ upvotes, it's gonna stand strong regardless.

I feel like this one was written just to spite of all the SCPs that were allowed to roam freely around their facilities.
Greatness is dead and I killed it. Sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. +1002. When I wrote this 7 years ago I never could have guessed this article would be as popular as this. Thanks to everyone who has voted and/or commented on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everything about this freaks me out in just the right way. Well done dude. Well done.

+1

item.title: I know this is very very very old but this needed saying
item.content:encoded: Holy SHIT dude, this is AMAZING for your first skip.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Plaierwon, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never-mind, I made a mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why was the last comment 6 years ago?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent Skip. Very creepy, loved it, especially the fact that it was smart enough to fool the Foundation.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This creates a great atmosphere, and it's difficult to pull away from

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know that the Foundation doesn’t like to destroy SCPs but this thing just needs to be destroyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing that convinced me to +1 was definitely the vague implication that -B attempted to crawl into someone's womb. Just the way -B was created is creepy enough, but those redactions are at just the right places.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
God, this guy creeps me out so much. Enjoy!

https://youtu.be/WVkcEmCMQFs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's some more music to read this to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whee!

Teddies for everyone!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+800 wheeee

EDIT: ಠ_ಠ really

item.title: Re: Theory: 1048 is misunderstood.
item.content:encoded:
I dunno… I'm feeling quite compelled to consider this one as "acts cute to encourage someone to pick it up and take it home, especially a child, then becomes a living nightmare as soon as it's wormed its way in and everyone's guard is down".

Quite what the actual fuck it's doing other than doing a very sloppy (yet effective) and downright horrific job of replicating itself using whatever absolutely random materials it can find, like a hugely glitched version of the robots in Batteries Not Included, though…

… well, who knows, and frankly who cares. Drop a safe on it, on sight.

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent change to tags was reverted, as it introduced two incorrect tags ("deadly" and "un-contained" (with hyphen) are not tags used by the site).

Furthermore, the edit also removed the correct tags that were already present on the page.

Community members, if you are not familiar with tags, please do not add new tags yourself. We have a team of experienced taggers that handles the addition of new tags.

For older articles in particular, it is advised that one suggest the addition of the tag in the corresponding discussion thread rather than adding a tag themselves.

item.title: Omg
item.content:encoded:
1+

Great grammar

Creepy atmosphere

Good description

:D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Teddy Bear, you were my Teddy Bear, you were comforting and quiet, how did love become so violent?

"Teddy Bear, you were my Teddy Bear, everything was so sweet, until you tried to kill me…"

item.title: Re: Theory: 1048 is misunderstood.
item.content:encoded:
1. I never said it shouldn't be contained and stuff.
2. "Made of bowties and capable of creating black holes."
XDDDDDDDDDD I like your creativity.

item.title: Re: Theory: 1048 is misunderstood.
item.content:encoded: All of this could be correct, and it wouldn't matter. It's still capable of creating things that are extremely dangerous, and has no trouble with harming people to get materials. It needs to be contained, and it's still Keter because they have no idea how many other duplicates it could have made during its time in Foundation custody, or even before it was acquired. Because it's never been properly contained, the Foundation has no idea how difficult it might be to prevent 1048 from creating more duplicates, even in containment. It could use material from the floor or any restraints they try to use on it for all they know. Maybe SCP-1048-M, which is made out of the zip-ties they try to bind its arms with, is capable of breaking reality or creating black holes.

item.title: Theory: 1048 is misunderstood.
item.content:encoded:
I know this statement sounds stupid, but after being apart of this community for years without an account and reading this SCP over and over, I have come to a conclusion. So for one, when 1048 first came to the facility, they were extremely passive. But what happened? Well, considering that 2295 uses stuff to help hurt people, people assume 1048 uses organic matter to make creatures to hurt people. EXCEPT IT DOESN'T. One, 1048-C proves that 1048 can animate inorganic matter, meaning it is probably NOT related to 2295, or is it's opposite. Heck, it may be a more advanced version in a way, because it can create life! So that's out of the picture. Next I will prove it's innocence. So the whole reason this SCP is keter is because it has created three versions of itself that are dangerous, but in all instances, (Except for 1048-B possibly, but I assume the baby tried to crawl into the researcher's womb because you know, it's a fetus), it always seems that the SCP personnel are what caused the violence. For instance, when SCP 1048-A was created, 1048 simply showed it around the facility like a teacher or parent, probably giving it a tour so it could get used to the place. It was only when the SCP staff SURROUNDED the NEWLY CREATED entity that conflict happened. Note that 1048 never directly attacked anybody, and simply ran away in panic or for other reasons. I theorize that the staff threatened 1048-A, causing it to attack and then flee. Now, what did 1048 use to create this thing? A ear. That sounds disturbing and evil, but it doesn't appear to want to cause pain, as it only seems to take body parts when the host is asleep, and also seems to have the ability to duplicate an item it obtains, meaning it purposely tried to avoid as much harm as possible. It even left one ear on the researcher it "borrowed" from. I believe 1048 was probably "lonely," and desired to make a companion like itself, which it did. Why it chose an ear is unknown, but again, it did not take multiple ears which it could have, as SCP 1048-C is made of VARIOUS pieces of scrap metal, not the same one. Speaking of 1048-C, 1048-C seemed to have been threatened as well, as it only attacked when it began to be chased, it also is not mentioned that it clawed or chased people, but jumped through them. This shows the desperation and fear that 1048-C may have been experiencing. About 1084-B, I believe it may be some sort of vengeful act by 1048 towards the personnel. After all, 1048-A is technically 1048's own creation, just like a baby is the "creation" of a mother, and the personnel scared it. Who even said if 1048 knows where 1048-A is, what if 1048 lost 1048-A and became upset? It's also noted that 1048 had the mercy to not kill the fetus, but what it did was still disturbing and could NOT be without evil intent. I am not saying that 1048 is safe, but I suggest the personnel there should realize eventually why this all happened. Maybe if they show some sort of kindness towards instances of it when it's sighted, and not chase after it, maybe it will come back into containment and THAN can be put somewhere that can properly contain it.

(Also would like to mention there doesn't appear to have been any other attacks even though 1048 and 1048-A have been sighted multiple times and could have attacked the people who encountered it.)

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Hot damn! :D

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I like Joreth's idea, and I went ahead and made the change myself.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: That call is going to belong to the author, since they approved sirpudding's original proposal up thread and that is what got implemented. I think it works out either way, but what do I know?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
A week or two late, but I would like the 2295 link to be just an additional addendum - the way the article looks right now seems to imply that 2295 is an instance of 1048 (since the bolded sections go 1048-A, -B, -C, then 2295).

Suggesting changing this:

SCP-2295 - This object is similar, but nearly antithetical in function, to SCP-1048. Attempts to establish a common origin are ongoing.

To something like this:

Addendum 1048-1: SCP-2295 is similar, but nearly antithetical in function, to SCP-1048. Attempts to establish a common origin are ongoing.

Alternatively, I like sirpudding's suggestion with regards to adding it in as a researcher note before Dr. Carver's comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got here from Reap What You Sew (if that tale stays up, hopefully) and holy christ.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Alternative option, a researcher note:

SCP-2295 is nearly a perfect antithesis of this. Coincidence or common origin?
- Senior Researcher [insert name]

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The author hath spoken!

Once the timer expires, assuming nothing else changes, I'll clip in sirpudding's proposal. No need to include the Roget proposal link if you are not personally enthusiastic about it, and a better idea has been proposed via 2295.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
I was feeling sort of against any connection, but then I read the comments on 2295 and apparently I practically encouraged it back in 2015. I'm personally okay with sirpudding's proposal; it keeps things simple without making any definite conclusion.

And I am wholeheartedly for linking to Roget's tale if that connection can be made. I'm fine with the proposal link too, though less enthusiastic about it.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
What about just a lampshade:

Put:

SCP-2295: This object is similar, but nearly antithetical in function, to SCP-1048. Attempts to establish a common origin are ongoing.

Near the end, after the list of instances and a similar but reversed line to 2295.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Apart from being stuffed bears, they don't really have anything in common

  • 1048 makes teddy bears out of people parts
  • 2295 fixes people with plushie parts

As I said they are directly antithetical, which does suggest a common origin to me. The simplest being that 1048 built 2295, which is a mutant, or whatever process that made 2295 went wrong and the first 1048 instance happened.

Other possibilities are balancing archetypes, ideological differences, or the split of an ur-bear into opposing halves.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Seems really convoluted. I don't think there's any reason to force this interaction.

I'd also be against anything hinting at a common origin for the pair. Apart from being stuffed bears, they don't really have anything in common. I like 2295, but I'm not sure it's the right link for this, nor am I really thrilled about the 001 proposal link.

ETA: On second thought, I take this back.

If anything, I'd suggest linking to a tale if we have 'em for an inline link. If you're going to go with an addendum, I'd focus on an attempt to capture 1048; hell if I can think of a suitable skip for that though.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm. Here's my attempt at it:

+ show block
– hide block

Put this at the end of the article.

Incident 1048-27: During a usual personnel transfer between Site-24 and Site-37, SCP-1048 entered a shipping truck without being noticed. Upon arrival, SCP-1048 entered Storage Wing-25 and [REDACTED], causing the loss of ██ personnel and causing major damage to SCP-1048.

SCP-1048 evaded capture from the responding MTF after constructing a copy of SCP-1048-B, and entered into another shipping truck that drove to Site-24. SCP-1048's current whereabouts are still unknown.

Thoughts would be nice

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
By all means show me what you got.

I try to generally be as non-invasive as possible with my crosslinks, especially on particularly popular skips, hence the inline link.

Floor's open if you think an addendum is more sufficient.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
They can make entire addendums if it's appropriate.

But yea, agreeing with MacLeod, the 2295 link should be more natural.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Tsk. Tbh I feel like the 2295 link should be more… subtle? Smooth? Smooth.

Can wikiwalk crew make substantial edits? Or just inline links?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Proposal updated.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I've been saying for a very long time: evil twin.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Huh. You'd think SCP-2295 might be one of the bears built by builder bear, except, you know, a non-murderous variety.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
There's its antithesis, the bear that does surgery and replaced organs with functional plushie prosthetics

ETA: SCP-2295

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below (sirpudding proposal). Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Ah yes, Builder Bear. For an article so popular, I'm surprised by how few tales/references to it exist in the larger wiki. One article that does reference it, however, is Roget's Proposal, which can work for a non-invasive in-line link within Dr. Carver's first note. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

-This is not a joke. We have no idea what SCP-1048's full capabilities are. Who knows how many of the damn things are out there by now? - Dr. Carver

To this:

-This is not a joke. We have no idea what SCP-1048's full capabilities are. Who knows how many of the damn things are out there by now? - Dr. Carver

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: Re: Picture source?
item.content:encoded: The pic is just from a teddy bear image search I did back then. I couldn't find the original image anywhere a few years ago when the site was going through the process of properly crediting the sources for photos. I did several reverse image searches too; not sure if anyone on staff found a source either.

item.title: Picture source?
item.content:encoded:
I kinda want a teddy bear like the one in the picture. Where is the picture from?

my fingers are crossed that the picture isn't one he took and that its from a teddy bear shop place.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well done. Gave me the shivers while I was reading this although I knew it had no chance of actually existing… Or does it?

item.title: Re: +600
item.content:encoded: Aww, I don't think anyone but me has celebrated an upvote milestone on this SCP since it first hit +100. Thank you!

In celebration, I dedicate this song to you:

Teddy Bear by Melanie Martinez

You were comforting and quiet
How did love become so violent?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do you still think Teddies are cute?
+1 all the way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs that deconstruct SCP cliches just make me so happy! This is coming from someone who likes 131!

item.title: Re: A Funny Text I Sent
item.content:encoded: Neat! Not sure if I've seen that thread before. Those warning notices are pretty cool.

item.title: Re: A Funny Text I Sent
item.content:encoded: Oh.
